source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Russian-Greek church, Kadiack [i.e., Kodiak], Alaska
description: 1894 photograph of Holy Resurrection Russian Orthodox Cathedral in Kodiak, Alaska. Originally established in 1794 by Russian Orthodox monks, the congregation is the oldest continuously serving Orthodox community in North America. The church building visible in this photograph predates the 1943-44 replacement that still stands today.
alt: 1894 sepia photograph of the Russian-Greek Orthodox church at Kodiak with two steeples and a white picket fence
